g.text: matches performance of tokenized methods like llama for the first time.
how to train directly on bytes, as it is prohibitively expensive
easy to control FLOP cost by controlling patches size
inconsistent and non contextual patching of similar information
cannot handle all languages and domain
train small decoder model to predict next entropy???
global boundary: if entropy of a byte is > "theta_g"
relative boundary: if entropy of current - previous bytes > "theta_r"
byte encoder -> latent transformer -> byte decoder
multi headed cross attention for patching
max pooling to init 1st cross attention in byte encoder
lr 1e-4, linear qarm up 2k steps, cosine decay
better than previous byte level models
BLT entropy patching better than BLT space patching.
BLT models are robust to noise and understands character level much better
min 300k hash embedding table is important to match BPE performance
close to BPE models at large scale
better scaling by increasing both patch and model size
